text.value: Laboratory Report
Laboratory studies

Epithelial cells [#/area] in Urine sediment by Microscopy high power field

Mucus [Presence] in Urine sediment by Light microscopy
Bacteria [#/area] in Urine sediment by Microscopy high power field
Bacteria identified in Urine by Culture

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The findings indicate a urinary tract infection (UTI) with significant bacteriuria (E. coli >100,000 CFU/mL), positive nitrites, leukocyte esterase, elevated leukocytes, and presence of blood in urine. Glucosuria is noted, which may warrant evaluation for hyperglycemia or diabetes. Low urine specific gravity suggests dilute urine. No casts are seen. Clinical correlation and prompt treatment of UTI are recommended; further assessment for underlying glycemic control is advised.

text.value: Tom Hayward

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
